summary:
In textbooks, the FDI World Dental Federation system is predominantly featured because it has broad international recognition. This systematic approach ensures consistent communication across different regions and professionals.
The Universal system starts with '1' at the top-right of an adult’s jaw but isn't as extensively covered or standardized compared to other methods like FDI. To directly address your query concisely:

In medical texts, the universally adopted tooth-numbering system is that recommended by the FDI World Dental Federation; this comprehensive yet internationally accepted format predominates over others such as the simpler Universal scheme found less frequently detailed in academic materials. \boxed{F
